results.title: Bombers to celebrate '70s premiership heroes
results.description: Noradjuha-Quantong will welcome back a host of former stars as part of a reunion to mark the 50th anniversary of Noradjuha’s record- breaking four premierships in a row from 1975 to 1978 in the former Central Wimmera Football League. The reunion, which will also commemorate the Noradjuha flags in 1966 and 1956, will be held on July 11 at the Horsham City Oval during the Bombers' clash with traditional rivals Harrow-Balmoral. The game was originally scheduled for Quantong, however recent heavy rainfall has meant club officials have shifted the game to Horsham. City Oval holds fond memories for the 1966 side, scene of the club's one-point premiership win over Horsham seconds. Event organiser Carmel Officer said that while the reunion was in honour of the premiership sides, all people who were involved in the former Noradjuha club, which merged with Quantong to create Noradjuha-Quantong in 1997, were welcome to attend. She said she was hopeful of a good turnout. "We've had a lot of interest from people and we're expecting a lot to travel back for the occasion," Mrs Officer said. "The reunion has specifically scheduled against Harrow-Balmoral, as Douglas-Harrow Miga Lake and Balmoral were two of Noradjuha's great rivals in the old league, so we hope a lot of people can catch up and share their memories of the good old days." Memorabilia from the era will be on display upstairs at the Horsham Football Club clubrooms and there will be speeches from those involved in the club during the 1970s, including premiership coach Bernard Noonan. Noradjuha-Quantong president Austin Smith said the club would be wearing its red and green "Grassparrots" strip to honour the original Noradjuha jumper. "It's going to be a big day for the club," he said. "Big thanks to the Horsham Rural City Council and the Horsham Football Club for being so accommodating in allowing us to transfer the event."

results.title: Backlash grows after Trump claimed he asked FIFA to overturn World Cup red card
results.description: US president Donald Trump's comments about influencing a World Cup decision have fuelled debate over politics and sport.

results.title: CHEATED! Egypt cry foul after FIFA World Cup loss to Argentina
results.description: Egypt coach Hossam Hassan claimed his side had been "cheated" out of a place in the FIFA World Cup quarter-finals.

results.title: Beekeeping as a hobby - why it's a great choice for young men struggling with mental health or loneliness
results.description: Share article Print article At first glance, beekeeping might seem an unusual hobby to introduce to young men - a group often discussed in policy and research as being "at risk", of poor mental health and disengaged from society.
